methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $5.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $500M.

data.narrative:
Commonwealth Fusion Systems, a Series B climate and energy company founded by Bob Mumgaard, has raised $2.863B to date and carries a last post-money valuation of $5.0B+. The relevant comparables universe comprises one public company (LNZA) and eight private peers, with the peer set anchored by five same-stage companies: Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M), and Cemvita Factory ($120M).

Two valuation methods proved applicable. The stage-progression approach, applying a typical 1.9x Series B step-up multiplier to the $5.0B last post-money valuation, implies a forward value of $9.5B. The peer-median method, anchored to five same-sector and same-stage comparables, yields a median valuation of $310M, with a range spanning $120M to $500M. The disparity between these methods reflects material differences in capital intensity, time-to-revenue, and market positioning within the climate-tech category.

Triangulation across both methods supports a valuation range of $264M (low) to $10.9B (high), with a median of $4.9B. The wide spread reflects genuine uncertainty regarding whether Commonwealth's scale, technical risk profile, and go-to-market trajectory warrant a meaningful premium or discount relative to peer medians. Key uncertainties include commercialization timeline, capital requirements to next inflection, and the sustainability of Series B multiples in frontier-energy subsectors.
